---
title: "Python for Scientific Computing and Data Visualizations"
subtitle: "Open-source tutorials for everyone who want to use Python in their works"
---

## Welcome {#welcome}

Welcome to __Random Spaces!__ This blog is a celebration of the fascinating world of __computational chemistry__. I hope you find the content useful, and I hope you enjoy reading along and learning with me!

And me? Well, I'm __Dinda Gusti Ayu__. I just a person who interested in using __Machine Learning to learn the rules of Quantum Mechanics and Simulate Disordered Material systems__. As a beginner, this blog aims to documenting my learning journey. Behind that, I earned an MS in chemistry physics at Universitas Sumatera Utara, Indonesia where I studied the fundamental optical properties and defects modelling of direct band-gap semiconductors for materials research.

## What is Random Spaces?

Random Spaces is a free, open-source learning resources for students and researchers interested in applying Python to solve complex problems, to manage and tranform data, and to produce publication-quality figures. The tutorials here bridge the gap beetween condensed matter physics, chemistry and computational tools.

All materials are shared openly on my repositories ![Static Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/github-repositories-black?style=for-the-badge&logo=github&logoColor=black&logoSize=auto&label=github&labelColor=hex&color=black&link=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fdindagustiayu)


Let's get down with computational chemistry!
